tasks.title: Site refresh: real screenshots, feature tour, zoom-callout hero demo
tasks.notes: GUI screen capture isn't possible in this headless env, so the hero is generated by compositing a showcase document through the REAL engine: new `SiteAssets` executable target (Sources/SiteAssets/main.swift, run via `swift run SiteAssets`) → site/assets/hero.png (2880×1800). It exercises zoom callout + leader lines, arrow/highlight annotations, text, and a styled (corner-radius+shadow+rotation) layer — honest engine output, not a mockup. index.html reworked: app-window-framed hero, 6-card feature tour with keyboard shortcuts, 1.0 copy, og:image. First-launch notice kept accurate (ad-hoc signed; no Apple signing secrets configured in repo).

tasks.title: CHANGELOG curation + 1.0 release notes
tasks.notes: Authored the 1.0.0 CHANGELOG entry (user-facing, grouped by feature area: zoom callouts, annotations, transforms, layers, capture/export, macOS feel, perf) and stamped the release file set per the release skill: VERSION 0.1.0->1.0.0, site/version.json version 1.0.0. README 'early preview' line replaced with 1.0 framing. Tag/publish deferred to 8.3.

tasks.title: Release 1.0.0 via release skill; verify download + site
tasks.notes: v1.0.0 tagged (== VERSION) and pushed; GitHub release published with Photonz.dmg and the CHANGELOG-extracted notes; verified the asset is reachable (zlib/DMG bytes returned via authenticated gh api). PREFLIGHT was green (Scripts/test.sh 367 tests, Scripts/build-app.sh --dmg succeeds, 1.9M DMG). TWO ENVIRONMENTAL BLOCKERS, neither a release defect, both need the user: (1) ALL GitHub Actions runs (Release, CI, Deploy-site) failed in ~4s with 'recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased' — account billing. I published the release manually via `gh release create` to work around the dead Release workflow; the live site still shows 0.1.0 because the Pages deploy is billing-blocked (site/ in repo is correct; re-run site.yml once billing is fixed). (2) The public 'releases/latest/download/Photonz.dmg' link 404s anonymously because the repo is PRIVATE (v0.1.0's asset 404s the same way — pre-existing). To make the marketing-site download work for the public, make the repo public or host the DMG on a public bucket. See backlog.md.

tasks.title: Post-1.0 backlog triage (Windows amd64 evaluation, App Store?)
tasks.notes: Created docs/plan/backlog.md: triaged post-1.0 candidates with priorities. Top of list are the two release blockers surfaced in 8.3 (unblock CI/Pages billing; fix the private-repo public-download gap), then Developer ID signing + notarization (secrets scaffolded in release.yml but not configured), then Windows amd64 evaluation and Mac App Store distribution. No new build phase opened — these are a backlog, not committed scope.
